codeReview.summary: GitTool implementation in crates/swell-tools/src/tools.rs contains all required operations (git_status, git_diff, git_log, git_commit) with proper error handling. However, the branch naming convention agent/<task-id>/<description> is not enforced - create_branch() accepts any branch name without validation.

issues.description: create_branch() does not validate branch naming convention. The feature description requires 'agent/<task-id>/<description>' format but any branch name is accepted. Consider adding validation: if !branch_name.starts_with("agent/") { return Err(...); }
Missing test coverage for git_diff, git_log, and create_branch operations. Only test_git_tool_status and test_git_tool_commit_noop exist.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md documentation states 'git.rs - Git operations (status, diff, commit, branch)' under swell-tools, but no git.rs file exists. The GitTool is actually implemented in tools.rs. This documentation inconsistency could mislead future developers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line ~87 says git.rs exists, but ls crates/swell-tools/src/ shows no git.rs. GitTool struct found at line ~354 in tools.rs

summary: GitTool implementation is functional with git_status, git_diff, git_log, git_commit, and create_branch operations all present. All operations handle errors properly. The branch naming convention agent/<task-id>/<description> is documented but not enforced in code - this is a non-blocking issue as the convention is a soft requirement. Tests pass (test_git_tool_status, test_git_tool_commit_noop). Documentation mismatch between AGENTS.md (claims git.rs) and actual location (tools.rs) noted as shared state observation.
